title: Product Supply Specialist (Customer Logistics)
location: Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR – Greater China
job_description:
Job Description
• Be part of Customer Supply Chain team which provides superior service to customers/retailers.
• Hypercare daily order and delivery to deliver excellent fulfilment
• Lead data insights from regular reports of OSA (on-shelf-availability), import/export transportation cost, and proactively work on improvement
• Expect to utilize P&G system and mange data maintenance
• Expect to be involved in a lot of multi-functions and external communication
Are you ready to take the challenge? We invite you to join us!
What we offer you:
• You will have business responsibilities from Day 1 – You will start of meaningful work from the beginning. Over time, as you expand your impact on the business, your responsibility and ownership will also quick grow.
• You will receive continuous coaching & mentorship– We are passionate about our work. We will make sure you receive both formal training and as regular mentorship from your manager and others.
• You will work in a dynamic and respectful work environment – We live our Purpose, Values, and Principles daily. We value every individual and encourage initiatives promoting agility and work/life balance.
What we are looking for:
• A minimum of a bachelor’s degree from an accredited university, any major.
• Candidates with experience in supply chain field or retail operation will be preferred.
• Positive attitude and strong willingness to learn, strong problem-solving skills
• Experience with Microsoft Excel is a must, other digital tool will be plus.
• Experience with 3rd Party Logistics management will be plus
• Language proficiency: English, Cantonese, Mandarin
• Ability to collaborate effectively within a multicultural environment.
This is important to note:
• You will receive the online assessment invitation in 1 day after you completed the application successfully.
• Immigration sponsorship is not available for this position.
• P&G welcome people with disabilities. P&G is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate against individuals on the basis of race, color, gender, age, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, citizenship, marital status, disability, HIV/AIDS status, or any other legally protected factor.

title: Logistics & Warehouse Operations Supervisor
location: Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR – Greater China
job_description:
Key Responsibilities
• Supervise and manage daily logistics operation to ensure smooth execution for daily replenishment shipment between multiple Distribution Centres, cross-docking operation, and last-mile delivery from HK warehouse to customers
• Maintain detailed documentation of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), workflows, and Working Instructions (WIs) to ensure consistency and quality
• Optimize warehouse layout, fulfilment flow and inventory management
• Manage relationships with logistics partners, couriers, and warehousing providers to uphold service standards
• Govern compliance and quality standard of all warehouses with local regulations and company internal policy, maintain best practices in product handling, storage, and transportation
• Collaborate with both internal departments, external partners to troubleshoot, implement process improvements resolve any logistical issues, support execution of cross functional projects
• Lead daily/weekly meetings with operational team and service providers to quick fix any urgent issues and barriers to sustain quality service to customers.
• Monitor and achieve the monthly and quarterly logistics KPI, analyse and report to management
• Sets operational expectations with 3PL Partners, regular monitoring on their standard and opportunity gaps
• Gatekeep compliant payment from P&G to 3PL Partners based actual service received and rate card standard, supervise timely payment to 3PL Partners
• Utilize SAP and P&G system
What we offer you:
• You will have business responsibilities from Day 1 – You will start of meaningful work from the beginning. Over time, as you expand your impact on the business, your responsibility and ownership will also quick grow.
• You will receive continuous coaching & mentorship – We are passionate about our work. We will make sure you receive both formal training and as regular mentorship from your manager and others.
• You will work in a dynamic and respectful work environment – We live our Purpose, Values, and Principles daily. We value every individual and encourage initiatives promoting agility and work/life balance.
What we are looking for:
• A minimum of a bachelor’s degree from an accredited university, any major
• Proven experience (5+ years) in the logistics industry and managing logistics vendors, preferably in the FMCG industry, ideally 1-2 years in supervisory role
• Understanding of Greater Bay Free Trade Zone operation will be plus
• Proficient in MS Office and SAP (WMS) is a must
• Language proficiency: English, Cantonese, Mandarin
• Frequent travel (day trip) between HK and Greater Bay area is expected
• Positive attitude and strong willingness to learn, strong problem-solving skills
• Ability to collaborate effectively within a multicultural environment
This is important to note:
Each applicant can only apply to ONE position
, please carefully consider which function and position you would like to apply to.

title: Warehouse System and Operation Specialist
location: Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR – Greater China
job_description:
Job Description
First-Level Support and Troubleshooting: Provide first level of technical and functional support and troubleshooting to end-users
System Implementation and Customization: Support UAT (User Acceptance Test) and validate system functionalities before and after go-lives, conduct functional tests and ensure correct technical implementation of system requests and improvements
Project Support: Work with Global or Regional Project or IT Team, collaborate with Operation Team, and represent end-users community as key person to translate operation needs into system solutions
End-User Training: Create job aids and conduct trainings to end-users
On-going Maintenance: Conduct regular system audits and ensure data integrity
Continuous Improvement: Identify opportunities for process improvements and system enhancements
Process Improvement: Contribute to the continuous improvement of operation processes and execution
Documentation: Help develop and document validation, change management and corrective / preventative action based on operation requirements
Nonconformity Identification: Learn to identify, document, and track system and operation issues with the aim to supporting development of resolution
Continuous Learning: Participate in training sessions and workshop to improve skills and knowledge to support operation
Job Qualifications
A minimum of a bachelor’s degree from an accredited university, any major
At least 1 year experience with SAP system is a must
Proficient in MS Office is a must
Experience in quality management, supply chain, or warehouse operations will be plus
Language proficiency: English, Cantonese, Mandarin
Big affinity with software systems and process handling
Positive attitude and strong willingness to learn, strong problem-solving and communication skills
Ability to collaborate effectively within a multicultural environment
This is important to note:
Each applicant can only apply to ONE position
, please carefully consider which function and position you would like to apply to.

title: Field Sales Supervisor - SKII
location: Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR – Greater China
job_description:
Job Description:
The Field Sales Supervisor plays a crucial role in ensuring we have perfect in-store execution. The role has mainly responsibilities focus in Hong Kong market. The role is responsible for managing and building the business for all assigned
counters to meet and exceed sell-through targets, perfect in-store fundamentals in line with SK-II brand equity, and drive BC Operations by ensuring BCs in respective stores excel in their responsibilities.
BUILDING THE BUSINESS
Achieve respective stores in identified markets yearly sell-thru targets
Responsible for preparation of monthly statistical, scorecards and market reports to Account Manager timely as needed
PERFECT IN-STORE EXECUTION
Following the Key Merchandising Principles per Planogram guideline (10-3-1)
HPP perfect execution: Ensuring on recruiting promoter, promotion/second site planning and set up, displays, stocks and GWP and liaising with necessary agencies
Ensuring all counters/tools are clean, neat and in good working condition
Ensuring on healthy stock inventory per the assortment list and check through OOS across all SKUs in key doors once a week
Ensure all testers are sufficient supply always for all locations
BC OPERATIONS
Work closely with customer/agency to track sales performance against goal & provide feedback & inputs to deliver on monthly targets for BCs
Work closely with BC Trainer to identify training needs specific to SK-II brand & product knowledge for all BCs at respective counters
Work closely with customer/agency to support recruitment activities to ensure BC team are fully staffed
Work closely with customer/agency to ensure BCs follow the grooming guideline and understand deployed SOPs to uphold SK-II brand equity
Ensure BC understands monthly deployment of store priorities and initiatives
Counter coaching for all BCs including generic staff if needed
Qualifications:
3 years or above working experience Beauty/Skincare/Prestige brands’ Travel Retail sales operation.
Exhibits Leadership skills and strong customer service orientation / attitude.
Strong data analytical skills.
Ability to work under pressure and deal with multiple responsibilities.
Strong interpersonal skills. Fluent in verbal and written Mandarin/Cantonese Chinese and English
This is important to note:
Each applicant can only apply to ONE position
, please carefully consider which function and position you would like to apply to.

title: Senior Brand Manager (Media Role)
location: Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR – Greater China
job_description:
Key responsibilities:
Media & Data strategy development:
• Lead strategy development and holistic planning for a portfolio of brands and a range of target segments across online and offline media channels and in both HK and TW markets
Data-driven media innovation:
• Drive the media innovation agenda: identify testing opportunities, oversee the execution and scale up successful pilots. Understand relevant emerging technologies and solutions, and test them and bring them to life as appropriate. Lead the global and local learning agenda
• Continue to lead the data and programmatic capability development and take it to the next level.
Media Investment optimization:
• Oversee budget optimization and media analysis and guide the brands to define the right media budget allocation to achieve the best media results. Define and drive optimization programs.
• Identify media best practices and reapply to brands to improve media performances
Media performance measurement:
• Establish a framework for performance measurement including KPIs and benchmarks. Monitor in-campaign and post-campaign performance deliver both business and media objectives at the right cost.
Media & Data partnerships:
• Establish and maintain partnership with agencies, publishers, tech partners and data providers to execute marketing campaigns. Partner with Purchases on annual negotiations
Build Media & Digital capability:
• Build the capability across all the P&G brands present in Hong Kong & Taiwan via training and introducing appropriate agencies to the region.
Media stewardship and governance:
• Oversee and manage the processes related to directing, influencing or monitoring the actions of agencies to ensure business objectives are met in line with the contractual agreement and in a manner consistent with laws and P&G policies. Define the regional media proof of performance process in alignment with global guideline, execute risk-based governance, monitor compliance and address risks.
Qualifications Required:
• 3-10 years of experience in a corporate media role, with proven success in leading brand media strategy and campaign planning.
• Proven leadership experience in building and guiding core teams, focusing on self-learning in social and digital trends.
• Strong judgment in navigating complex social environments and driving innovation.
• Ability to influence and engage business leaders through data-driven insights and strategies.
• Excellent project execution skills, ensuring high-quality content delivery from concept to completion.
• Strong analytical skills with a focus on data-driven decision-making.
• Experience in agency management: engage and empower agencies to identify service improvement opportunities.
• Ability to build strong relationships across teams and with leadership.
• Agile mindset with a keen interest in consumer engagement.
This is important to note:
Each applicant can only apply to ONE position
, please carefully consider which function and position you would like to apply to.

title: Senior Key Account Manager
location: Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR, Hong Kong SAR – Greater China
job_description:
Are you having a real passion for winning? Are you intrigued by the idea to build long-term business relationships with our customers? To develop and negotiate plans which create a win-win for our shoppers, our customers and our Company? Do you have an ambitious spirit and a willingness for learning?
If you have proven analytical skills and are looking for a rewarding job which will allow you to grow personally and professionally, then come be a Key Account Manager at Procter & Gamble to strengthen your mastery!
Working in sales, you will influence our customer's decisions in critical business areas by using conceptual selling techniques and data-based presentations. For consumer accounts, this involves making recommendations on assortment, shelving, pricing, and merchandising strategies, based on consumer research that gives us insight into what drives shopper purchase behavior.
Your Role
As a Key Account Manager, you´ll directly take meaningful and impacting responsibilities as of day 1.
You´ll be in charge for maintaining and expanding a long-term partnership with your customers by continuously proposing solutions and plans that meet their objectives. To do so, you’ll develop a keen understanding of our product categories and markets.
You will need to:
• Be the point of brand and category expertise with our customers - serving as a trusted business advisor.
• Help by innovating new solutions to complex business challenges.
• Own and manage significant customer accounts.
• Participate on diverse, multi-functional teams and eventually lead and manager others. Your responsibilities would include:
• Developing, selling, implementing, and evaluating business plans to meet or exceed financial goals
• Collaborating with multi-functional teams (Marketing, Finance, Logistics, Operations, among others) to develop strategies and plans
• Managing and improving distribution, pricing, shelving, and merchandising
• Translate Business Plans into actionable selling stories for the customer
• Develop productive working relationships with key customer contacts and sell mutually beneficial business plans
• Turn raw data into actionable conclusions
• Use analytical tools and integrate various data systems to develop new insights
• Provide input into retailer category strategy
What We Offer You:
• Responsibilities from Day 1 – You will start working on key brands from the beginning. Over time, as you expand your impact on the business, your responsibility and ownership of a brand will quickly grow.
• Recognized state of the art marketing skills – We will constantly help you improve your marketing knowledge and management abilities.
• Continuous coaching & mentorship– We are passionate about our work. We will make sure you receive both formal training and regular mentorship from your manager and others.
• Dynamic and respectful work environment – We live our Purpose, Values, and Principles daily. We value every individual and encourage initiatives promoting agility and work/life balance.
We are looking for:
• With the completion of a bachelor’s degree or above in an accredited university with at least 3 years of experience in managing Key Account
• Excellent visionary, strategic and courageous leadership skills.
• Demonstrated ability of critical thinking and problem solving.
• Strong in communication and collaboration with others.
• Proficiency in English (written and spoken). Cantonese is a must.
This is important to note:
Each applicant can only apply to ONE position
, please carefully consider which function and position you would like to apply to.
